---
title: "Getting Started With R And Quarto"
author: "John Lockwood"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# This is some markdown

Of course. Let's try a simple R code block:

```{r}
print("Hello!")
```

And now some python, using our installed Conda environment, which RStudio graciously agreed to read from environment.yml? We can hope...

```{python}
import numpy as np
print("Hello!")
print("Numpy version:", np.__version__)
```

## Some Important Quarto Notes:

CMD-SHIFT-ENTER runs a single cell.

When creating an executable cell, beware of "Insert / Code Cell". That's just a markdown cell wrapping the code in the usual Markdown code cell. What you want is "Insert Executable Cell".
